witnesses.description: A direct proof witness where p and p -> q establish q.
A counterexample to the invalid converse step from p -> q to q -> p.

checks.claim: The listed assignment witnesses the direct proof pattern p, p -> q therefore q.
checks.notes: The validator checks p is true, p -> q is true, and q is true under the listed assignment.

checks.claim: There is no Boolean assignment separating p -> q from not q -> not p.
checks.notes: The validator enumerates all four assignments and finds no counterexample to contrapositive equivalence.

checks.claim: There is no Boolean assignment with p -> r and not p -> r both true while r is false.
checks.notes: The validator enumerates all four assignments and checks the finite proof-by-cases pattern.

checks.claim: There is no Boolean assignment satisfying p, p -> q, and not q.
checks.notes: The validator enumerates all four assignments and rejects the contradictory premise set.

checks.claim: The converse inference from p -> q to q -> p is valid.
checks.notes: The validator checks p -> q is true while q -> p is false, so the alleged proof rule is invalid.

checks.claim: General natural-deduction soundness and proof reconstruction require future Lean resources.

data.target_theorem_shape: soundness of direct, contrapositive, cases, contradiction, and contradiction-elimination proof rules for a formal proof system
data.future_checker: Lean module or kernel-checked proof reconstruction without sorryAx dependencies

checks.notes: The finite Boolean rows are checked; general proof-system soundness remains a proof-assistant horizon.
